title: Parodia elachisantha
intro: Parodia elachisantha, also known as the Pachycereus elachisanthus, is a cactus species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its tall, columnar stem and vibrant yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Parodia elachisantha include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Parodia elachisantha include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues.
